name: node-semver
title: node-semver
description:
Semantic Versioning for Node.js
Test if version(s) satisfy the supplied range(s), and sort them.
Multiple versions or ranges may be supplied.
Program exits successfully if any valid version satisfies
all supplied ranges, and prints all satisfying versions.
.
This package provides the `semver` executable and the Node.js module.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side javascript engine.

arguments.description: Coerce version strings left to right (default)
arguments.raw: --ltr  Coerce version strings left to right (default)

arguments.description: Identifier to be used to prefix premajor, preminor,
arguments.raw: --preid  Identifier to be used to prefix premajor, preminor,

arguments.description: Coerce version strings right to left
arguments.raw: --rtl  Coerce version strings right to left

command_examples: semver component is Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER || (2**53 - 1); higher value
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use semver because Semantic Versioning for Node.js.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use semver as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `semver -h` as documented upstream for semver.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `semver 1.2.3 -i prerelease --preid beta` as documented upstream for semver.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `semver 1.2.4-beta.0 -i prerelease` as documented upstream for semver.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `semver 1.2.4-beta.1 -i release` as documented upstream for semver.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair semver with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `semver --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: --ltr
arguments.description: Coerce version strings left to right (default)

arguments.name: --preid
arguments.description: Identifier to be used to prefix premajor, preminor,

arguments.name: --rtl
arguments.description: Coerce version strings right to left

examples: semver component is Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER || (2**53 - 1); higher value
documentation.readme: semver - The semantic versioner for npm Install npm install semver Usage As a node module: const semver = require('semver') semver.valid('1.2.3') // '1.2.3' semver.valid('a.b.c') // null semver.clean(' =v1.2.3 ') // '1.2.3' semver.satisfies('1.2.3', '1.x || >=2.5.0 || 5.0.0 - 7.2.3') // true semver.gt('1.2.3', '9.8.7') // false semver.lt('1.2.3', '9.8.7') // true semver.minVersion('>=1.0.0') // '1.0.0' semver.valid(semver.coerce('v2')) // '2.0.0' semver.valid(semver.coerce('42.6.7.9.3-alpha')) // '42.6.7' You can also just load the module for the function that you care about if you'd like to minimize your footprint. // load the whole API at once in a single object const semver = require('sem
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install node-semver. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `semver -h`. Further invocations from upstream: `semver 1.2.3 -i prerelease --preid beta`; `semver 1.2.4-beta.0 -i prerelease`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Semantic Versioning for Node.js.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: semver - The semantic versioner for npm Install npm install semver Usage As a node module: const semver = require('semver') semver.valid('1.2.3') // '1.2.3' semver.valid('a.b.c') // null semver.clean(' =v1.2.3 ') // '1.2.3' semver.satisfies('1.2.3', '1.x || >=2.5.0 || 5.0.0 - 7.2.3') // true semver.gt('1.2.3', '9.8.7') // false semver.lt('1.2.3', '9.8.7') // true semver.minVersion('>=1.0.0') // '1.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install node-semver
